description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring multiple large, rectangular photovoltaic panels mounted on a flat base. The panels are arranged in two distinct tilted arrays, each supported by visible structural frames, likely made of metal. The base platform appears to be constructed from a solid, possibly concrete or composite material, with additional elements such as a vertical cylindrical post and a rectangular utility box. The surface of the panels is dark with a grid pattern, characteristic of solar cells, while the supporting structures are metallic and robust. The installation includes various technical components, such as mounting brackets and possible electrical housings, but no visible cabling. The overall design suggests a fixed, ground-mounted solar array suitable for urban or industrial environments.
description: A ground-mounted solar energy installation with two tilted arrays of photovoltaic panels on a solid base, supported by metallic frames and technical components.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the installation, with both tilted solar panel arrays and the central technical components clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the arrays, the tilt angle of the panels, and the vertical cylindrical post at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back sides of the solar panels, the supporting frames, and the technical box on the left.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the alignment of the panels, the technical box, and the cylindrical post in the foreground.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 30-35 degrees, facing a consistent direction, with a fixed orientation.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with visible metallic (likely aluminum or steel) support frames and a solid base platform.
attribute_array_configuration: Two separate arrays, each with 2-3 large panels, arranged in parallel rows on a shared base.
